reasoning: The recent jump in ALB stock to ~$84 is primarily attributed to a "temporary" halt at a CATL lithium mine in China, resulting in a short-term supply bottleneck. Given the temporary nature of this specific catalyst, the stock is vulnerable to mean reversion in the near term once normal operations resume or if the initial market reaction is seen as overblown. The YFinance time-series analysis tool computed a combined probability of 0.42 based on linear and seasonal trends, which serves as my primary anchor for the near-term forecast (Sep 7). As the forecast horizon extends to 1-month, 3-months, and 6-months, the specific temporary effects of the August supply shock will dissipate. Under standard asset pricing dynamics, longer horizons represent increased variance and generalized uncertainty, meaning the predictive power of the current catalyst decays. In alignment with explicit instructions, my probability estimates regress toward 0.50 as the horizon extends further into late 2025 and 2026.
